Event: Amy reacts devastatedly when Bender bakes her a pony for brunch
Explanation: Her strong emotional reaction hurts herself while not helping anyone and arguably dampens the social situation.

Event: Amy hooks up with sensitive Kif
Explanation: They form a stable, mutually supportive long-term relationship that benefits both.

Event: Amy expresses distaste for bad boys and desire for a good guy
Explanation: Stated preference aligns with later healthy relationship with Kif and discourages harmful partners.

Event: Amy keeps her eyes on bad boys and flirts with them despite being with Kif
Explanation: She gets ego and excitement while undermining Kif’s feelings and the relationship.

Event: Kif breaks up with Amy over her bad-boy flirting
Explanation: He preserves some self-respect but loses a valued relationship, and Amy is also hurt.

Event: Amy starts a robosexual affair with Bender after breakup
Explanation: She gains pleasure and companionship with Bender but at some cost to Kif and social stability.

Event: Kif acts “bad” in his own way to make up with Amy
Explanation: He adapts to reconcile the relationship, boosting both his bond and her satisfaction.

Event: Amy and Fry remain close friends after breaking up
Explanation: They salvage a positive friendship that benefits both without romantic strain.

Event: Amy and Bender remain good friends and dance together after breakup
Explanation: They maintain a fun, supportive friendship despite past romance.

Event: Amy accuses Leela of not being ladylike enough
Explanation: She reinforces her own feminine self-image while passively undermining Leela.

Event: Amy in Leela’s body overeats because it’s not her own body
Explanation: She enjoys indulgence at the direct physical expense of Leela’s body.

Event: Amy’s overeating leaves Leela’s body gigantic by episode’s end
Explanation: She has enjoyed herself but leaves Leela with severe physical consequences.

Event: Amy’s appetite is killed after seeing Fry and Leela have sex in others’ bodies
Explanation: The traumatizing sight harms her while at least stopping her harmful overeating.

Event: Amy repeatedly removes Zoidberg’s restraints despite being injured
Explanation: She keeps harming herself and enabling danger with no gain.

Event: Amy dates Fry because of shared ditzy, rebellious traits
Explanation: Both enjoy companionship and bonding over similarities.

Event: Fry dumps Amy for “suffocating” him
Explanation: He regains space at the cost of hurting Amy and ending a functioning relationship.

Event: Amy loves and commits to raising Kif’s children despite difficulty
Explanation: She accepts hard work for strong emotional and familial rewards for herself and the kids.

Event: Amy latches onto her swamp-emerged children immediately years later
Explanation: Her full emotional investment benefits her happiness and their sense of being loved.

Event: Amy brings attention to her cleavage-window outfit as an explanation for time skips
Explanation: She offers a (joking) explanation and displays self-aware humor with no harm.

Event: Amy’s parents attempt to forcibly split up her relationship with Bender
Explanation: They damage Amy’s autonomy and happiness without gaining anything clear themselves.

Event: Amy defends Kif as not scrawny when her parents insult him
Explanation: She supports her partner’s dignity and asserts her own choice.

Event: Amy fails at haggling by confusing it with bidding at auctions
Explanation: She likely overpays, helping the seller at her own expense.

Event: Amy flirts with all men
Explanation: She gains attention and fun, but can create awkwardness or strain existing relationships.

Event: Amy suggests an end-of-the-world orgy when Earth faces death
Explanation: She seeks pleasure and distraction while potentially destabilizing group dynamics and commitments.

Event: Amy changes clothes in preparation for the proposed orgy
Explanation: She readies herself for pleasure at minor potential social cost before others lose interest.

Event: Amy accepts motherhood by end of “Kif Gets Knocked Up a Notch” out of love
Explanation: She sacrifices some party lifestyle for deep relational and familial rewards.

Event: Amy becomes fully invested as a parent when the children emerge
Explanation: Strong commitment enhances her fulfillment and the children’s well-being.

Event: Bender burns Amy’s longer hair back to familiar style by burping fire
Explanation: He likely finds it amusing but causes cosmetic harm and distress to Amy.

Event: Amy dates creatures that aren’t even humanoid
Explanation: She pursues broad romantic/sexual experiences, generally benefiting both sides without shown harm.

Event: Amy kisses Leela in Bender’s Game
Explanation: In-context playful intimacy seems mutually enjoyable with no lasting downside shown.

Event: Amy proposes an “end-of-the-world orgy” again when Earth faces death
Explanation: She tries to engineer group pleasure with little regard for others’ boundaries or consequences.

Event: Amy cursing in Cantonese when angry
Explanation: She vents and feels better while verbally harming or insulting others slightly.

Event: Amy wears a tracksuit to rebel against her parents’ demand to be ladylike
Explanation: She asserts independence and comfort at the cost of antagonizing her parents’ wishes.

Event: Amy frequently insults Leela yet still socializes and invites her to wedding
Explanation: She maintains a friendship but often gains amusement or superiority through barbed comments that hurt Leela.

Event: Amy abandons Leela to go for “coffee” with guys
Explanation: She gains romantic/sexual opportunity while disregarding her friend’s feelings and time.

Event: Amy acts more masculine to fill the void of her father wanting a son
Explanation: Her self-presentation is driven by unmet parental approval, limiting her authenticity without clear benefit to him.
